text:
Hospitals inParisare reeling under the strict new security systems that have been put in place for the2024 Olympic Games.

Access to the medical centres in areas of the capital close to sporting venues have become fraught with difficulty, potentially endangering people's lives.

Security has been stepped up in preparation for the beginning of the sporting festival, that starts on Friday 26th July.

The city has been divided into red and grey zones, which require special permits and passes to enter.

READ MORE:Nightmare for Macron as Paris Olympics in 'crisis' after new strikes announced



While the fences and roadblocks are making life difficult for ordinary Parisians, medical professionals are facing a nightmare.

An ambulance driver complained that it was taking much more time to pick up and drop off patients, many of whom require urgent treatment.

"It takes 50 minutes to 1 hour to make a journey to drop off or pick up a patient, and that's a problem because the man we're picking up has been there waiting for 2.5 hours," he told the channel BFMTV.

"We do what we can, but it's hard to get around," he added despairingly.

Authorities have introduced a Game Pass with a QR code for people who need to enter the security zones.

Don't miss:Anne arrives in Paris ahead of 2024 Olympics after Zara misses out on spot

Don't miss:Paris Olympics chaos as half a million tickets still unsold ahead of Games

Don't miss:Paris Olympics fear 'major threat' from Russia just days before Games

Two perimeters have been set up to secure the competition venues and the opening ceremony.

The first is the red zone: located near the competition venues, it is accessible to pedestrians and cyclists without a Games Pass. Only vehicles require a pass to enter.

The grey zone, on the other hand, is closed to pedestrians, cyclists and vehicles from 18 July to 1pm on 26 July. To enter, people must have the Games Pass QR code.

A man recovering from heart surgery was forced to take the metro rather than. A taxi to get back home, due to the access difficulties.

His wife told BTMTV: "The hospital secretary told us to take the metro.

"Ideally, we'd like to find a taxi, but she said that would be complicated, so I'm trying to find out because the metro seems too complicated."

Excitement is growing in France's capital as the clock ticks down on the Opening Ceremony.

We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

Paris 2024 will mark the first time in history of the Olympic Summer Games that the official opening is held outside a stadium.

Instead of familiar images of athletes marching out along an athletics track, guests and viewers will be treated to a colourful river parade through the heart of the French capital.

title: Panic in Paris as Olympics cause huge problems for city's hospitals | World | News | Express.co.uk
author: John Varga, Maria Ortega
description: While the fences and roadblocks are making life difficult for ordinary Parisians, medical professionals are facing a nightmare.
